title: Mastering API Design
subtitle: The Complete Guide to Architecture, Security, and Scale
author: Wordsus Team
description: Master the art of designing scalable, secure, and developer-friendly APIs. This comprehensive guide covers RESTful principles, GraphQL, gRPC, security patterns, documentation, and the full API lifecycle for modern software ecosystems.

chapters.title: Introduction to Application Programming Interfaces
chapters.description: Learn what APIs are, their role in modern software ecosystems, and how they drive the global API economy.

chapters.title: The Evolution of Web APIs
chapters.description: Trace the history of APIs from RPC and SOAP to the RESTful revolution and the rise of modern protocols.

chapters.title: Core Architectural Styles
chapters.description: Explore request-response vs event-driven models, statelessness, caching, and layered system architecture.

chapters.title: Network Fundamentals for API Designers
chapters.description: Understand the OSI model, TCP/IP mechanics, DNS, and TLS/SSL foundations essential for API communication.

chapters.title: Resource-Oriented Design
chapters.description: Master the principles of identifying domain resources, modeling relationships, and managing resource states.

chapters.title: HTTP Methods and Status Codes
chapters.description: Deep dive into safe and idempotent methods, state updates, and standardizing success and error status codes.

chapters.title: Data Representation and Formats
chapters.description: Study JSON, XML, content negotiation, and the role of hypermedia (HATEOAS) in API discoverability.

chapters.title: URI Structure and Naming Conventions
chapters.description: Establish best practices for endpoint naming, pluralization, and the effective use of query vs path parameters.

chapters.title: Exploring GraphQL
chapters.description: Solve over-fetching problems with GraphQL schemas, queries, mutations, and performance optimization.

chapters.title: Event-Driven APIs and Asynchronous Patterns
chapters.description: Implement webhooks, Server-Sent Events, and WebSockets for real-time and asynchronous communication.

chapters.title: gRPC and Protocol Buffers
chapters.description: Leverage HTTP/2 and Protocol Buffers for high-performance, strictly typed microservices communication.

chapters.title: API Pagination and Filtering
chapters.description: Design scalable pagination (offset vs cursor), complex filtering logic, and standard sorting strategies.

chapters.title: Error Handling and Problem Details
chapters.description: Build consistent error payloads using RFC 7807 and craft actionable messages for developers.

chapters.title: API Documentation and Developer Experience (DX)
chapters.description: Maximize API adoption with OpenAPI specs, interactive consoles, and high-quality documentation.

chapters.title: SDKs and Code Generation
chapters.description: Evaluate the value of SDKs and automated code generation for ensuring idiomatic support across languages.

chapters.title: Authentication and Authorization Foundations
chapters.description: Master OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, JWTs, and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) for securing APIs.

chapters.title: Advanced API Security Best Practices
chapters.description: Protect systems from injection, CSRF, and CORS issues through threat modeling and penetration testing.

chapters.title: API Versioning Strategies
chapters.description: Manage API lifecycles with URI, header, or media type versioning and clear deprecation policies.

chapters.title: Rate Limiting and Quotas
chapters.description: Defend against abuse and DoS attacks with rate limiting algorithms and tiered usage plans.

chapters.title: API Gateways and Microservices
chapters.description: Understand the role of API Gateways, the BFF pattern, and service meshes in modern architectures.

chapters.title: Testing and Mocking APIs
chapters.description: Ensure reliability through unit testing, contract testing, and using mocks for parallel development.

chapters.title: Monitoring, Analytics, and Monetization
chapters.description: Monitor API health with logging and tracing, and explore strategies for API business monetization.

parts.title: Part I: Foundations of API Design
Part IV: Designing for the Consumer
